description_long: This is a cylindrical wooden apothecary jar with a snugly fitting lid, featuring a small knob on top for easy removal. The jar is adorned with three carved horizontal bands near the top and bottom, adding a decorative touch to its otherwise smooth surface. On the front, there is a large, white, oval label bordered in black, displaying the text 'Radix Sambuc[uli] Korzen bzu' in elegant, cursive script. The wood grain is visible throughout, giving the container a rustic, antique appearance. The jar appears to be hollow, designed to store medicinal substances, likely in powdered or dried root form. There are no visible hinges, locks, or tamper-evident features, and the label is the only distinguishing mark.
description: A cylindrical wooden apothecary jar with a fitted lid and a white, cursive-labeled front, used for storing medicinal roots.
description_view_0: Front view showing the white label with cursive text and the decorative carved bands on the wooden surface.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the smooth, uninterrupted wood grain and the edge of the label just visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden surface with carved bands, no label or markings visible.
description_view_3: Angled view showing part of the label and the jar's rounded form, highlighting the lid's knob.
